title: Sprint 3 midpoint on canary results, refinement of the fix, and progress on reconciliation.

metadata.focus: Follow-up on sprint 3 with real data from the canary and final refinement of the payment front.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Midpoint of sprint 3. I want concrete data: what is the canary telling us, how is reconciliation going, and if the fix is really getting close to being done.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The rollout of the fix in wallets has significantly improved the timeouts, but we've detected a rare case in the Apple Pay callback. It doesn't break everything, but it does require us to fine-tune that branch before closing.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: That fits with what we saw on the frontend. The problem is no longer the long general timeout, it's that specific callback route.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: So, the fix is no longer open for the same reason as in sprint 2, but we can't call it resolved yet either.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: We've made a lot of progress with reconciliation. The stable export now allows us to detect real duplicates versus apparent retries, and support no longer has to look at all the cases manually.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: That means to me that the duplicate blocking is effectively resolved and now we're in the consolidation phase, not the discovery phase.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The canary from the alternative provider at five percent is performing better in latency than the current provider, but I still don't want to expand it. I need another week of data and to close the Apple Pay callback first.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: That makes sense. If you expand traffic before closing that corner, you're mixing two changes at once.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: I would prepare the criteria to increase from five to twenty percent, but without executing it yet.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: We'll leave it as a possible next step, not as a closed action for this midpoint.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: The important thing for support is that we're already identifying real duplicates in less than an hour. That completely changes the operation.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: We close the midpoint: the fix is being fine-tuned in Apple Pay, reconciliation is already functioning at a minimum, and the canary is still alive but without expansion yet.

expected_items.title: Correct the Apple Pay callback before expanding the rollout of the fix in wallets.
expected_items.summary: Resolve the specific branch that continues to show strange behavior after the rollout of the fix.

expected_items.title: Define the criteria to expand the canary of the alternative provider from 5 to 20 percent.
expected_items.summary: Prepare thresholds and conditions for expansion without executing the change yet.

expected_followups.matched_history_title: Extend the rollout of the retry fix to the wallets adapter and measure its real impact.
Automate a minimal reconciliation of duplicate payments with the stable settlement export.
Launch a canary of the alternative provider on 5 percent of wallet traffic.
